1.gl: properly, to glitter from afar, i.e. to be eminent (as a superintendent, especially of the Temple services and its music); to be permanent

1.gl: remote, literally or figuratively, of place or time; specifically, precious; often used adverbially (with preposition)

7.gl: properly, to hedge about (as with thorns), i.e. guard; generally, to protect, attend to, etc.
7.kj: beward, be circumspect, take heed (to self), keep(-er, self), mark, look narrowly, observe, preserve, regard, reserve, save (self), sure, (that lay) wait (for), watch(-man).
